REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK NOTICE OF PETITION AND PETITION OF DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. THIS ACTION SHALL PROCEED IN ARBITRATION. UPON COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION, PARTIES SHALL PROCEED IN COURT ON THE EQUITABLE RELIEF AVAILABLE UNDER THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE AND UCL VIOLATIONS IF SUCH RELIEF IS WARRANTED BASED ON THE ARBITRATION OUTCOME. PARTIES' CONTRACT CONTAINS A VALID BINDING ARBITRATION PROVISION THAT HAS NOT BEEN WAIVED AND IS NOT UNCONSCIONABLE. THE COURT CONSIDERED SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES REGARDING HASTY. THE COURT FURTHER CONSIDERED ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES. THE COURT NOTES THAT MINIMAL EVIDENCE (SELF-SERVING DECLARATION ONLY) WITHOUT ANY SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION WAS SUBMITTED ON THE ISSUE OF PLAINTIFF'S INABILITY TO PAY ARBITRATION FEES. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED (AFTER THE COURT ALLOWED SUBMISSION OF ANY EVIDENCE PARTIES WISHED TO SUBMIT) THE COURT MAKES A DETERMINATION THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT CARRY ITS BURDEN REGARDING INABILITY TO PAY. ARONOW V. SUPERIOR COURT (2022) 76 CAL.APP.5TH 865. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTED, SHEILA PHAM CSR 13293, CALENDAR-LA@VERITEXT.COM., 415-274-9977 (D501)

Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendant Tri Counties Bank To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Nelson Goodell, Esq., The Goodell Law Firm, 415495-3950 in-person for plaintiff
Thomas Mouzes, Esq., Boutin Jones Inc., 916-321-4444 via zoom for defendant

Mr. Goodell contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK NOTICE OF PETITION AND PETITION OF DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED. This action shall proceed in arbitration. Upon completion of the arbitration, parties shall proceed in Court on the equitable relief available under the causes of action for wrongful foreclosure and UCL violations if such relief is warranted based on the arbitration outcome. Parties' contract contains a valid binding arbitration provision that has not been waived and is not unconscionable. The Court considered supplemental briefing submitted by the parties regarding Hasty. The Court further considered additional evidence submitted by the parties. The Court notes that minimal evidence (self-serving declaration only) without any supporting documentation was submitted on the issue of Plaintiff's inability to pay arbitration fees. Based on the evidence submitted (after the Court allowed submission of any evidence parties wished to submit) the Court makes a determination that Plaintiff did not carry its burden regarding inability to pay. Aronow v. Superior Court (2022) 76 Cal.App.5th 865.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported, Sheila Pham CSR 13293, Calendar-la@veritext.com., 415-274-9977 (D501)
